quotes.text: The customer is always right about the problem. They're almost always wrong about the solution.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you ask users what they want, they'll say a faster horse. Your job is to build the car.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Watch what users do, not what they say. They'll tell you they use your product daily. Their login history tells a different story.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The user who submits a bug report with screenshots and steps to reproduce is worth their weight in gold.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Requirements are like nose hair: everyone has them, nobody wants to see them, and they keep growing.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The problem with requirements is that they're written by people who don't have to implement them.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every 'easy' feature request is just code someone else doesn't have to write.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Edge cases aren't edge cases when you have enough users. They're Tuesday.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The user who says 'it's just a simple feature' has never written a line of code in their life.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you build exactly what the customer asked for, they'll complain it doesn't work the way they actually need it to.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The most important user is the one who's about to leave. Build for them first.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: User feedback is data. But data without context is just noise. Always ask 'why?'
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best product decisions come from observing users, not surveying them. Surveys measure opinions. Observations measure behavior.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every product team says they're 'user-focused.' Then they prioritize internal metrics over user needs.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best onboarding is invisible. The user shouldn't even know they're being onboarded.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Users don't read. They scan, they click, they get frustrated. Design for that reality.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best error message is one that tells the user what to do next. The worst tells them what the developer did wrong.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your product has a 'getting started' guide that's 47 pages long, your product is too complicated.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best feature is the one you don't build. Every feature is a future maintenance burden and a current cognitive load.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Happy path development is what you do when you've never met a user. Users don't take happy paths. They take confused paths.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to understand your users is to be confused by your own product for 5 minutes. Then fix everything that confused you.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Users don't want a better tool. They want a better outcome. The tool is just a means to that end.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best product decisions come from understanding the user's actual workflow, not the one you imagined they had.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If a user has to read a manual to use your product, your product has failed.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The user who gives you critical feedback is your best friend. The user who silently leaves is your biggest risk.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every feature you add creates a new path for users to get confused. Add features like you'd add rooms to a house: with purpose.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your power users will forgive bugs. Your new users won't. Design for both.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best user research is watching someone use your product without telling them what to do. The discomfort reveals everything.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your FAQ page is a list of your product's failures. Fix the underlying issues and delete the FAQ.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to understand user needs is to be your own user for a week. You'll discover things no survey would tell you.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your most important feature request is the one users haven't asked for yet, but would change their life if it existed.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you can't explain your product in 30 seconds, your marketing page can't explain it in 30 seconds either.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to reduce user confusion is to reduce options. Every choice you give the user is a chance for them to choose wrong.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your users are smarter than you think. Stop holding their hand. Give them tools and get out of the way.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your product's biggest flaw is the one your users complain about most. Fix that first.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every product should pass the 'Grandma test': could your grandma figure it out? If not, simplify.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best product teams listen more than they talk. Your users know more about their problems than you do.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your user interface should be like a good joke: if you have to explain it, it's not good.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to reduce churn is to make your product indispensable. Not by locking people in. By being too useful to leave.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your product roadmap should be based on user needs, not competitor features.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best product managers are the ones who can say 'no' to 99% of requests and still be loved.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your product should be so intuitive that the first-time user experience feels like coming home.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to understand user needs is to observe, not ask. Users will tell you what they think they want. Observing tells you what they actually need.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every product has a 'magic moment' — the instant the user gets it. Find it. Optimize for it.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Your most valuable product feedback comes from the users who almost left but stayed. Find out why they stayed.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best product features are the ones users create themselves. Build a platform, not a product.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your users aren't inviting others, your product isn't good enough. Viral growth starts with genuine satisfaction.
quotes.author: based
